text.title: Rerank Before Reasoning Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every rerank-before-reasoning spec MUST enforce.

rule.statement: When k_retrieved > k_final AND domain-specific re-ranking is feasible, the spec MUST insert a cross-encoder reranker stage.
rule.rationale: Embedding similarity is a proxy for relevance; reranker reads the actual query-candidate pair.

rule.statement: k_retrieved MUST be > k_final * 3 to give the reranker enough recall headroom.
rule.rationale: Below 3x, the reranker has nothing useful to promote.

rule.statement: The reranker model MUST be a cross-encoder (BGE-reranker, Cohere Rerank, mxbai-rerank). Bi-encoders are forbidden as rerankers.
rule.rationale: Bi-encoders are what the retrieval stage already used; switching to cross-encoder is the whole point.

rule.statement: The spec MUST capture pre-rerank vs post-rerank MRR@k_final on a held-out set; deploy only if MRR delta > 0.05.
rule.rationale: Otherwise rerank adds latency for nothing.

rule.statement: On reranker timeout, the spec MUST fall back to embed-only top-k_final, NOT block the request.
rule.rationale: Reranker failure shouldn't bring the product down.
